observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the:
a. MAO to (i) submit a revised proposal for the fund transfer amounting to P550,000.00 since the purchase of Buffalo Murrah is no longer feasible; and pursued the procurement of the Buffalo Murrah. (ii) submit the required LRs/FURs to BFAR MIMAROPA for the proper recording of the said liquidation reports and eventual reconciliation with their books of accounts; Implemented
b. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to remit/refund the unexpended balance amounting to P13,280.00 to the DA or the BTr, pursuant to Section 4.9 of COA Circular No. 94-013; and
c. Municipal Accountant to reclassify the misclassified accounts to their proper liability accounts for fair presentation of the account balances in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction:
Not Implemented No revised proposal was submitted to the source agency since the Management still pursued the procurement of the Buffalo Murrah.
Implemented The required FUR was submitted to the source agency, hence records between the two agencies were already reconciled.
Implemented The unexpended balance was adjusted in December 2024.
Implemented The Municipal Accountant drawn Journal Entry Voucher to reclassify the affected accounts.
observations.reason: No revised proposal was submitted to the source agency since the Management still pursued the procurement of the Buffalo Murrah.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the
Municipal Mayor effectively cause for the optimal utilization of the 20% DF thru proper planning and timely execution and/or implementation of the PPAs to enumerate maximum use of the allotted resources for the realization of the objectives of the fund and the benefits that will redound to the beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction: Not The current year appropriation of the 20% Development Fund registered an 80.74 percent utilization rate. However, the overall utilization rate was pulled down to 43.61 percent due to the low utilization of the continuing appropriation which accounted for only 18.98 percent.
observations.reason: The overall utilization rate was pulled down to 43.61 percent due to the low utilization of the continuing appropriation which accounted for only 18.98 percent.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the
Municipal Mayor require the:
a. BAC and the TWG to strictly comply with the pertinent provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 on the procurement of goods and infrastructure projects through public bidding and other modes of procurement with regards to posting requirements and particularly the completeness of supporting documents, among others, to enhance transparency and to establish the regularity of the procurement process; b. Municipal Accountant to check the completeness and propriety of the supporting documents prescribed under Sections 9.1 and 9.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001, to ensure compliance with pertinent sections of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and to establish the regularity, validity and propriety of the disbursements before processing claims for payments; and c. The Municipal Engineer to require the concerned contractors to post the required signboards/billboards at the project location containing relevant information of the ongoing infrastructure projects.
observations.managementAction: Not The BAC, TWG and the Management exerted their best efforts to comply with the laws, rules and regulations, particularly the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and COA Circular No. 2012-001. Moreover, the Management exerted its best efforts to comply with the requirements of the said laws, rules and regulations. The Municipal Accountant submitted the lacking documents.
observations.reason: The submission or attachment of some documents was overlooked by the concerned municipal officials and employees.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the
Municipal Mayor facilitate the imposition of liquidated damages on infrastructure projects with delayed completion and require the Municipal Engineer to: a. Prepare and submit the lacking engineering documents to facilitate technical review and evaluation of four infrastructure projects; b. Ensure that the computation of ABC is in accordance with DPWH DO No. 197; and c. Direct the concerned contractor/s to correct and rectify the deficiencies in three infrastructure projects and to submit to the Audit Team the compliance report thereon, together with the pictures before, during and after rectification of the deficiencies noted, otherwise, require the payment of the corresponding costs of the defects from the contractor/s.
observations.managementAction:
Not The required engineering documents were not submitted due to oversight of the Municipal Engineer.
Implemented The Municipal Engineer followed the afore-cited DPWH DO in the preparation of the ABC.
Implemented The Municipal Engineer sent demand letters to the concerned contractors requiring them to correct and rectify the noted deficiencies.
observations.reason: Liquidated damages was not imposed upon the contractor, due to oversight of the concerned municipal officials/employees.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Supply Officer, Inventory and Disposal Committees, and other departments concerned to exert earnest effort in substantiating the already issued and distributed inventories totaling P5,100,927.70, and if already documented, prepare the necessary accounting entry to derecognize the inventory accounts with corresponding adjustment to Government Equity account, or if warranted, apply the guidelines on the proper disposition of dormant accounts provided in COA Circular No. 2023-008.
observations.managementAction: Not For the Drugs and Medicines for Distribution account, the LGU conducted the procedures provided in COA Circular No. 2023-008 and submitted a request for approval to write-off/derecognize dormant accounts from the books on December 27, 2024. For the other issued items in other inventory accounts, they remained unsubstantiated and reported in the FS due to absence of documents.
observations.reason: For the other issued items in other inventory accounts, they remained unsubstantiated and reported in the FS due to absence of documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Designated Supply Officer to prepare and maintain Stock Cards to record all the receipts and issuances of supplies and materials and ensure that these property records are reconciled with the Supplies Ledger Cards maintained by the Accounting Office.
observations.managementAction: The Designated Supply Officer still failed to prepare and maintain the Stock Cards for all the receipts and issuances of supplies and materials.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Acting Municipal Treasurer (a) to intensify collection strategies such as information campaign, barangay caravan, and service of notices of delinquency and persuade taxpayers to take advantage of early payment discounts and henceforth increase the collection of real property taxes; and (b) together with the Municipal Assessor to recheck all the records of the taxpayers and the status of their real properties for proper valuation and to identify the reasons for delinquencies for appropriate action.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented The Treasurer’s Office conducted tax caravan in barangays in October 2024 and continued to send notices to unpaid and delinquent taxpayers. However, the collection efficiency rate was still low due to late conduct of tax campaign caravan in barangays; difficulty in locating the whereabouts of the owners of some properties with delinquencies; and some owners no longer reside in the Municipality and the occupants or relatives often refuse to accept the notices if their names are not indicated therein. The Acting Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Assessor have started the rechecking of records of taxpayers, wherein they discovered that some lot owners were not aware that their properties had tax declarations thus, were unable to pay their obligations. However, the rechecking of records of other taxpayers is still ongoing.
observations.reason: However, the collection efficiency rate was still low due to late conduct of tax campaign caravan in barangays; difficulty in locating the whereabouts of the owners of some properties with delinquencies; and some owners no longer reside in the Municipality and the occupants or relatives often refuse to accept the notices if their names are not indicated therein.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor to (a) complete and submit to the Audit Team the lacking documents in compliance with Section 4(6) of P.D. No. 1445 and Item 13.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001; and (b) register before the Registry of Deeds the acquired parcel of land in order to secure a TCT/CLT in the name of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented Some of the lacking documents were already submitted to the Audit Team, except for the parcellary survey plan and the related documents thereto, due to the failure of procurement for the survey of the parcel of land. The registration of the acquired parcel of land before the Registry of Deeds could not be made unless the survey documents were secured.
observations.reason: The registration of the acquired parcel of land before the Registry of Deeds could not be made unless the survey documents were secured.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and it was agreed that the Municipal Mayor will instruct the GAD Focal Person to (a) accomplish the GAD Agenda and furnish a copy to the Audit Team; and (b) ensure proper computation of the scores reflected in the HGDG Checklists to avoid misleading the users of the report as well as the GPB and GAD AR.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented The GAD Agenda was not yet formulated and submitted to the Audit Team due to heavy workload. Errors in the computation of scores in the HGDG/PIMME Checklists were still noted because these were prepared by the implementing office and were not reviewed/checked prior to the preparation of the GPB and GAD AR. Both were revised in Observation No. 8 in Part II of this report.
observations.reason: The GAD Agenda was not yet formulated and submitted to the Audit Team due to heavy workload.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and it was agreed that the Municipal Mayor will require the Municipal Engineer to submit a written explanation on the variances between the Contract Cost and ABC vis-à-vis the COA cost estimate.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented Inquiry with the Municipal Engineer revealed that they have already sent a demand letter to the contractor to return/refund the noted amount of variance and the equivalent amount of uninstalled electric lamp post. However, these were still not refunded/returned by the contractor as at December 31, 2024.
observations.reason: However, these were still not refunded/returned by the contractor as at December 31, 2024.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

a. Municipal Accountant to consider the conditions stated in COA
Circular No. 2016-005 in the writing-off of receivables aged 10 years or
more wherein settlement/collectability is remote; and

b. Municipal Treasurer to continue with the issuance of demand letters to
borrowers to recover the past due receivable accounts to ensure cash
availability for future beneficiaries and continuity of the loan
programs.

We reiterated that the Municipal Mayor:

a. Strictly enforce the concerned former AO to liquidate/settle her
outstanding cash accountabilities amounting to P75,285.17
immediately;

b. Instruct the concerned officials to fast-track the processing of the
documents of the deceased AO and submit the same to the Audit Team
for the writing-off of the receivable account balance amounting to
P42,833.82; and

c. Require the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the Advances to
Officers and Employees and Due from Officers and Employees account
balances to Other Receivables account for fair and correct presentation
of the account balances in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

a. Inventory Committee to complete the conduct of the actual physical
count of all its Inventories and PPEs for an accurate and reliable
reporting and submit the corresponding physical inventory reports; and

b. Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the
discrepancies in the Inventories and PPE account balances totaling
P0.405million and P1.717million, respectively, and make the necessary
adjustments for fair and accurate presentation of the account balances
in the financial statements.

We reiterated that the Municipal Mayor require the:

a. Municipal Agriculturist to submit a revised proposal for the fund
transfer amounting to P0.550million since the purchase of Buffalo
Murrah is no longer feasible;

b. Concerned department heads to facilitate the implementation of PPAs
for funds from DSWD, PhilHealth and PGOM to be fully utilized and
for the Municipality and the constituents to enjoy the immediate use
and benefits that could be obtained therefrom; and

c. Municipal Accountant to maintain and update the subsidiary ledgers,
which should reconcile with the Due from LGUs account of other LGUs
and the controlling accounts in the general ledger, pursuant to Section
10 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume II.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal
Development Council (MDC):

a. Reprogram the idle PPAs under the Continuing Appropriations to
another developmental PPAs that are relevant to the current times and
needs of the Municipality and its constituents; and

b. Ensure the optimal utilization of the 20% DF, including the Continuing
Appropriation, through timely implementation of the PPAs to
maximize resource allocation and fulfill the objectives of the fund,
ultimately benefiting the intended beneficiaries.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

a. Municipal Accountant to ensure that claims are duly supported with
complete and proper documentation before processing claims for
payment to establish the validity, regularity and propriety of the
disbursements; and

b. Concerned department heads to ensure that erasures/alterations and
handwritten entries in the DTRs are properly countersigned before
forwarding the same to the Human Resource Management Officer for
review.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

a. Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and its Technical Working Group
(TWG) to strictly comply with the provisions of the updated 2016
Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 on the procurement of goods and
infrastructure projects, with regards to posting requirements and
completeness of supporting documents, among others, to enhance
transparency and to establish the regularity of the procurement
process; and

b. Municipal Accountant to check the completeness and propriety of the
supporting documents prescribed under Section 9.1 of COA Circular
No. 2012-001 to ensure compliance with pertinent sections of the
updated 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and to establish the legality,
validity and propriety of the disbursements before processing claims
for payments.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant to derecognize and properly account in the books the above-
demolished properties upon receipt of the COA Decisions on the submitted
requests for relief from accountability.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the
Supply Officer, Inventory and Disposal Committees, and other departments
concerned to exert earnest effort in substantiating the already issued and
distributed inventories, and if already documented, prepare the necessary
accounting entry to derecognize the inventory accounts with corresponding
adjustment to Government Equity account, or if warranted, apply the guidelines
on the proper disposition of dormant accounts provided in COA Circular No.
2023-008 dated August 17, 2023.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned
employees to expedite the submission of the lacking and revised documents
relative to the Request for Approval to Derecognize Dormant Accounts for
proper evaluation and disposition thereof. Also, require the Head of the
Inventory Committee and Municipal Accountant to explain in writing the
discrepancy in the Fuel, Oil and Lubricants Inventory account.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant to exert effort in substantiating the nature of the fully depreciated
PPEs in the Other Supplies and Materials Inventory account and prepare the
necessary accounting entry therefor for the fair presentation of the affected
accounts in the FS, or if warranted, apply the guidelines on the proper
disposition of dormant accounts provided in COA Circular No. 2023-008.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the
Designated Supply Officer to prepare and maintain SCs to record all the receipts
and issuances of supplies and materials and ensure that these property records
are reconciled with the Supplies Ledger Cards maintained by the Accounting
Office.

a. Acting Municipal Treasurer (i) together with the collecting officers to
strictly observe the existing rules and regulations governing collections
and deposits and strengthen the internal controls placed in the handling
and recording of collections and deposits; (ii) direct the LO to be more
circumspect in the acknowledgement of remittances in terms of accuracy
of the data and amount; and (iii) to reflect the amounts remitted by the
LO in the RCD to ensure completeness of information; and
b. Municipal Accountant to adjust the balance of the Cash Local Treasury
account and other accounts affected by the errors noted above and ensure
that the collections and deposits are accurately recorded in the books of
accounts.

For the long outstanding and dormant accounts, we recommended that
the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to (a) send
confirmation letters to the concerned agencies to validate the existence of the
receivable accounts in their books; (b) continue to send demand letters for
the overdue accounts and render a report on the status of the demand
letters sent to enforce collection thereof to protect the interest of the
government; and (c) consider the applicability of the guidelines on the
proper disposition of dormant accounts provided in COA Circular No. 2023-
008 for fair presentation of receivable accounts in the FS. This is without
prejudice to the management’s responsibility to continue to exert efforts to
collect the said receivables.
For the cash advances, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. require the AOs (i) with unliquidated cash advances based on records to
immediately refund the excess cash advances and ensure that at year-end,
all cash advances are liquidated or refunded; and (ii) to liquidate their
cash advances within the prescribed period provided in Item 5.1 of COA
Circular No. 97-002;
b. avoid the granting of cash advances in favor of other employees or
provide additional controls to ensure the immediate and full liquidation
thereof; and
c. instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the certification that
previous cash advances have been liquidated and accounted in the books
as one of the required documents in the grant of cash advance pursuant
to Item 1.1.4.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 to ensure that no additional
cash advance is granted unless the previous cash advance given to
him/her is first settled or proper accounting thereof is made.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct
the Acting Municipal Treasurer to (a) intensify tax collection strategies such as
timely conduct of information campaign and barangay caravan, service of
notices of tax delinquency and encouraging taxpayers to take advantage of the
amnesty program and early payment discounts; and (b) in coordination with the
Municipal Assessor, review the records of the taxpayers and the status of their
real properties for proper valuation and identify the reasons for delinquencies
for appropriate action.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant to (a) conduct analysis on the nature of the fund transfers not yet
utilized for a long period to properly establish the obligation of the Municipality
to the source agencies; and (b) facilitate the return of the unexpended balances
of the fund transfers pertaining to completed projects. It is also advised that ORs
be secured for the refunds made.

We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Mayor
instruct the:
a. LDRRMO to (i) exercise diligence in the preparation of the LDRRMFIP
to ensure accurate presentation of data; (ii) properly classify the
expenditures in the LDRRMFIP and ensure that such classifications are
reconciled with other related documents; (iii) see to it that the priority
projects and activities identified in the LDRRMFIP are promptly
implemented; and (iv) revise the “History of Repair” form to include the
necessary data to accurately identify and track the repair and
maintenance of each vehicle and equipment;
b. Municipal Accountant to strictly ensure that claims for traveling
expenses and repair and maintenance of vehicles and equipment are
supported with Certificate of Travel Completed and History of Repair,
respectively; and
c. BAC Chairperson to ensure the submission of POs to the Audit Team
within the prescribed period pursuant to Item 3.2.1 of COA Circular No.
2009-001.

With the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct
the GAD Focal Person to (a) give extra time and effort in the formulation of the
GAD Agenda and furnish a copy to the Audit Team; (b) review/recheck the
computation of the scores reflected in the HGDG/PIMME Checklists to ensure
the correctness of the scores reflected in the GPB and GAD AR; and (c) submit
the GPB to the Audit Team within the period provided in Item V of COA
Circular No. 2014-001.

We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Mayor
instruct the (a) HRMO to indicate in the job order contract the specific
function/s and/or job description/s of the job order workers hired and ensure
that said contract is acknowledged by the personnel hired; and (b) Municipal
Accountant to ensure that the grant of cash advances for the wages of job order
personnel is supported with proper documentation pursuant to Item 1.1.1 of
COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor make a proper representation with the DENR Provincial Office for the final disposition of the unexpended balance amounting to P5,656,744.25 and require the: a. Municipal Accountant to make proper representation with the AGDB and establish agreement with the bank to furnish the Municipal Accounting Office with the monthly debit and credit memos for the timely recording of reconciling items; and b. Municipal Treasurer to determine if the stale checks in the hands of the payees are subject for replacement, otherwise cancel and report to the Municipal Accounting Office for proper adjustment in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction:
Implemented The Management already made representation with the DENR. The said source agency agreed to the LGU’s proposal and required the LGU to submit various necessary documents.
Implemented The Management made representation with the bank, hence the reconciling items were properly recorded in the books of accounts.
Implemented Various stale checks were cancelled and properly adjusted in the books of accounts.
observations.reason:
The Management already made representation with the DENR. The said source agency agreed to the LGU’s proposal and required the LGU to submit various necessary documents.
The Management made representation with the bank, hence the reconciling items were properly recorded in the books of accounts.
Various stale checks were properly adjusted in the books of accounts.

We recommended that the
Municipal Mayor strictly enforce the concerned former AO to liquidate/settle her outstanding cash accountabilities immediately.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the
Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Budget Officer to consider in the Annual or the Supplemental Budget an appropriation for the cost of the transfer of land titles in the name of the Municipality; b. Municipal Assessor to facilitate the titling of lots in the name of the Municipality to ensure ownership and control over the properties and to avoid any legal impediments due to possible third-party claims; and c. Municipal Accountant to identify the origin of P2,194.76 Land account balance under the SEF books.

Implemented Appropriation for the processing of land titles was included in the CY 2024 Annual Budget.
Implemented The land title for the municipal compound was already secured while the titling for the lot in Brgy. Burol is already in process.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the
Municipal Mayor, being the Chairperson of the LDRRM Council, cause for the optimal utilization of the LDRRMF to strengthen the resilience of the constituents to disasters and climate change impacts; and require the LDRRM Officer to include under a separate caption, the list of PPAs charged against the STF in the LDRRMFIP, pursuant to Section 5.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-02 dated September 12, 2012.
observations.managementAction: Implemented The 70% Mitigation Fund registered 90.57 percent utilization rate during the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor create an Inventory Committee who will conduct the complete physical count on all the PPEs of the Municipality following the guidelines set forth under Section 5 of COA Circular No. 2020-006 and require the: a. Inventory Committee to complete the conduct of the actual physical count of all the PPEs and submit the corresponding reports; and b. Municipal Accountant to reconcile the discrepancies and make necessary adjustments.
observations.managementAction: Implemented The Inventory Committee was created in June 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to check the completeness and propriety of supporting documents prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-001 before processing claims for payments to establish the legality, validity and propriety of the disbursements.
observations.managementAction: Implemented The Municipal Accountant exerted best efforts to comply with the requirements of laws, rules and regulations, particularly COA Circular No. 2012-001.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the: (a) accountable officers to file a request for relief from accountability for the loss of properties caused by fire, theft, force majeure, or other causes as stipulated in the MNGAS for LGUs and COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992; and (b) Inventory and Disposal Committees, in coordination with the concerned departments, to initiate the necessary actions to promptly derecognize the demolished properties from the PPE accounts, in accordance with the procedures outlined in COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.

We also recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. Municipal Accountant to prepare an adjusting entry to correct the above error in recording in accordance with the description of accounts provided in Annex B of COA Circular No. 2015-009 for fair presentation of the affected accounts in the financial statements; 
b. Designated Supply/Property Officer (i) to prepare and maintain Property Cards for each class of PPE to record the acquisition, description, custody, disposal and other information about the properties; and (ii) the authorized Inspection Officer to indicate in the IAR the report number and the date when the said report was accomplished; and
c. Inventory Committee to properly accomplish the RPCPPE by identifying the accountable officer for each property and use the format provided in Annex 49 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume II so that the required data/information are reflected in the inventory reports.
observations.managementAction:
(a) Implemented (a) On October 16, 2024, the accountable officers (AOs) submitted their requests for relief from property accountability for the TB DOTS building and market building and stalls razed by fire.
(b) Implemented for the TB DOTS building and market building and stalls razed by fire.
(b) The Municipality conducted a physical count of PPEs on July 26 to August 2, 2024 in relation to the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts. After that, the AOs submitted requests for relief from property accountability.
(a) Implemented On October 1, 2024 and December 31, 2024, the Municipal Accountant prepared adjusting entries under JEV Nos. 2024-10-004827 and 2024-12-006317, respectively, to correct the said error.
(b) Implemented The Designated Property Officer is preparing and maintaining Property Cards for each class of PPE. (ii) Implemented The IAR contains a report number and date when accomplished.
(c) Implemented The accountable officer/custodian of inventory items was already identified in the RPCPPE.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to review thoroughly the nature of the fully depreciated and unserviceable PPEs in the Other Supplies and Materials Inventory account and prepare the necessary accounting entry thereof for the fair presentation of the affected accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office made accounting entries to adjust the unserviceable properties and the other item “tumbler”. However, the fully depreciated PPE remained unadjusted due to the absence of related documents to substantiate its nature.
observations.reason: However, the fully depreciated PPE remained unadjusted due to the absence of related documents to substantiate its nature.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Mayor will require the: a. Designated Supply Officer (i) and the authorized Inspection Officer to indicate in the IARs the report number and the date when these reports were accomplished; (ii) the Head of the requesting unit to indicate in the RIS the number of the report; and (iii) to consolidate the RIS and prepare SSMI and forward the same to the Accounting Unit as basis of the latter for the preparation of JEV to record the appropriate expenses; and b. Inventory Committee to properly identify in the RPCI the accountable officer/custodian of inventory items for proper identification of accountabilities.
observations.managementAction: (i) Implemented The report number and the date were already indicated in the report. (ii) Implemented The RIS number was already indicated in the report. (iii) Implemented The SSMI supported with RISs were already prepared and submitted to the Accounting Office. (c) Implemented The accountable officer for each property was already identified in the RPCPPE.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. instruct the MDRRMO to integrate in the LDRRMFIP the list of programs, projects and activities (PPAs) charged to the unexpended LDRRMF in the STF and prepare a revised or supplemental LDRRMFIP in case additional funds for DRRM are appropriated during the year pursuant to Item 5.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-002; and
b. require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the amount of unexpended LDRRMF transferred to the STF is accurate based on the actual expenditures.
observations.managementAction: Implemented The list of PPAs charged to the STF was already incorporated in the revised LDRRMFIP. On March 27, 2024, the Municipal Accountant made the actual transfer of the remaining unexpended LDRRMF of CY 2023 amounting to P1,277.15 to the STF. Moreover, the amount of unexpended LDRRMF for CY 2024 transferred to the STF on December 27, 2024 totaling P2,282,368.91 was correct.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the HRMO to limit the payroll deductions following the order of preference and incurrence as provided for in the GAA and henceforth ensure that the employees’ net take home pay is not less than the mandatory minimum amount.
observations.managementAction: Implemented The HRMO ensured that the net take home pay of employees is not less than the mandatory minimum amount. As at December 31, 2024, no employee has a net take home pay less than P5,000.00.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MPDC and the Municipal Engineer to fast track the implementation of one noted PPA under continuing appropriation which still remained unimplemented.
observations.managementAction: Implemented The project “Construction of Sanitary Landfill Facilities” funded by the CY 2021 20% DF was implemented in CY 2024.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to: 

cause for the approval of the revised project proposals of farm inputs for Barangays Agkawayan and Burol;
observations.reason: No follow-up was made with the DSWD Regional Office due to oversight of the concerned municipal officials.

observations.recommendation: ensure the prompt remittance of the unexpended balance from the completed project amounting to P13,280.00 to the concerned SA or the Bureau of the Treasury.
observations.reason: The unexpended balance of P13,280.00 remained outstanding due to oversight of the concerned municipal officials.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
