Based on the foregoing observations, we reiterated our recommendation
that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to:
a. exert extra effort to account the unreconciled items of two (2) PNB
accounts appearing in the BRS of the LBP accounts to faithfully
present the correct balances of the accounts;
b. prepare the necessary adjusting entries to record and correct the
unrecorded amounts and erroneous entries made in the Cash in Bank
account, respectively;
c. revert to proper Cash in Bank and payable accounts all Stale Checks;
and
d. prepare and submit the BRS together with the bank statements and
paid checks for all the Municipality’s bank accounts within the
reglementary period.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct
the Municipal Accountant to send demand letters for the overdue accounts and
render report on the status of the demand letters sent to enforce collection
thereof to protect the interest of the government. Section IV. General Guidelines
of COA Memorandum No. 2017-010 dated May 15, 2017 provides that: “x x x.
The Audit Team Leader (ATL) shall request from the Accountant proof of
exhaustion of all remedies to collect receivables and liquidate cash advances and
fund transfers as basis for the issuance of the final demand letter. There is
therefore a need for the Accountant to issue demand letter to the
AO/employee/recipient/NGOs to liquidate their due and demandable cash
advances/fund transfers.”
We also recommended that local officials expedite the submission of the
request for authority to write-off dormant receivable accounts, unliquidated
cash advances, and fund transfers of National Government Agencies (NGAs),
Local Government Units (LGUs) and Government-Owned and Controlled
Corporations (GOCCs) to the Commission on Audit in line with COA Circular
No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016, stating the reasons for write-off and
extent of follow-up made. The management should see to it that the request for
write – off is duly supported with the documents required in Item 8.3 of the
mentioned circular.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the BAC to:
a. furnish the Audit Team with a copy of the Contract of Agreement and
its attachments within five (5) working days from the execution thereof
as required under Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated
February 23, 2009;
b. observe strictly the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No.
9184 to ensure adherence to the principles of transparency,
competitiveness and public monitoring;
c. strengthen internal control over the evaluation of the bids and post-
qualification of bidders to ensure timeliness, completeness and veracity
of the post-qualified documents as reported in the Bid and Post-
Qualification Evaluation Reports;
d. submit the lacking documents cited above to the Audit Team to comply
with the requirements of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. 9184 and
address the deficiencies enumerated; and
e. prepare the required reports/documents with extra care to avoid
errors which could mislead readers and users of the reports.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant as the internal auditor,
see to it that supporting documents attached to the vouchers are accurate and
complete before processing the claims.

In view of the foregoing audit observations, we recommended that the
Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Disaster Risk and Reduction Officer (MDRRMO) to
prepare the LDRRMFIP in accordance with the format prescribed in
Annex A of COA Circular No. 2012-002. It should also include the list
of projects and activities charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of
previous years;
b. Municipal Accountant to effect corrections to the errors and over
transfer noted in CY 2017 as well as the over-transfer of fund in CY
2018 to properly present the correct balances of affected accounts;
c. Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere with the guidelines set forth
by COA Circular No. 2012-002 for proper accounting of the utilization
and transfer of LDRRM Fund as well as proper recording of various
transactions in the Special Trust Fund and Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445
and COA Circular No. 2012-001 for proper documentation of
transactions. She should also be guided by COA Circular No. 2015-009
dated December 1, 2015 in the proper recording of transaction in
accordance with Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards
(PPSAS); and
d. Bids and Awards Committee to strictly adhere to the requirements of
COA Circular No. 2009-001 and the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No.
9184 for the submission of contracts/purchase order and for
procurement of various goods and services of the municipality,
respectively.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor through
the MSWMB fast track the processing of various requirements for the operation
of a sanitary landfill in accordance with Sections 40 and 41 of R.A. No. 9003 the
soonest possible time to avert adverse environmental consequences.

Based on the foregoing, we reiterated our recommendation that the
Municipal Mayor:
a. advise the Municipal Treasurer to regularly remit the share of the
province in the SET in accordance with Section 9 of R.A. No. 5447;
and
b. instruct the Municipal Accountant, as the internal auditor, to see to it
that supporting documents attached to the vouchers are accurate and
complete before processing the claim. She should also be guided by
Annex B of COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015 in
recording transactions.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. require the strict and uniform implementation of the provisions of
COA Circular No. 97-002 on the grant, utilization and liquidation of
cash advances to all officials and employees of the municipality;
b. require all accountable officers and employees to settle/liquidate all
their outstanding cash advances by sending confirmation, notification
or demand letters. For unliquidated cash advances which are non-
moving for more three (3) years, exhaust all remedies to collect or

demand to liquidate the unliquidated cash advances, otherwise apply
for write off in accordance with the guidelines and procedures
prescribed by COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 29, 2016;
c. secure written approval from the Governor for any official travel
outside the province in accordance with Section 96 of R.A. No. 7160;
d. instruct the Municipal Accountant, as the internal auditor, to see to it
that supporting documents attached to the vouchers and liquidation
reports are accurate and complete before processing the claims and
observe the existing rules and regulations and rates of expenses and
allowances for official local and foreign travel of government
personnel; and
e. instruct the Municipal Accountant to properly classify each cash
advance transaction in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-009
and reclassify the balance of Advances to Officers and Employees
pertaining to the cash advances of accountable officers who were
already not in the service, retired or dead amounting to P88,095.24.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare a Journal Entry
Voucher to record the cancellation of stale checks and recognition of liabilities for the
fair presentation of the Cash in Bank and Payables account balances in the financial
statements.
The Municipal Accountant commented that she will prepare a Journal Entry
Voucher to record the cancellation of stale checks and will bring the matter to the BIR
officers as regards the five stale checks payable to the BIR.

We reiterated our audit recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct
the Municipal Treasurer, MSWDO and the MAO to exert their best effort to
collect long-outstanding receivables by (a) sending collection notice to concerned
debtors; and (b) instilling in the minds of future beneficiaries the need to return the
amount to ensure fund availability and continuity of the program. We also
recommended that the Municipal Accountant update their records and reconcile
the schedule of outstanding receivables with the ledger balance of Due from
NGOs/POs account to ensure correctness of the account balance.
Management has made several collection efforts to increase recovery of long
outstanding receivables with very little success. A continuous commitment to collect
loans receivables was assured by the Management for the year 2018.

We recommended that the Management enforce the preparation of PPMPs
by end-users for consolidation into an APP by the BAC Secretariat. See to it that
the APP is consistent with the Annual Budget, reflects the entire procurement
activity of the agency for the calendar year and have all necessary information
required under GPPB Circular No. 1-2009 dated 20 January 2009.
Management commented that they will require the end user units to prepare and
submit their respective Project Procurement Management Plans to support the Annual
Procurement Plan. They also committed to include all procurement in their APP, instead
of previous years’ practice of preparing the APP only for procurement of commonly
used supplies.

We recommended that the (a) BAC (i) conduct competitive bidding pursuant
to Section 10 of RA No. 9184; (ii) resort to alternative methods of procurements only
in highly exceptional cases provided in the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184; and (iii)
consolidate requisitions for one or more items needed at or about the same time; (b)
Municipal Accountant review the regularity and completeness of supporting
documents before processing claims for payment to establish their validity, legality
and propriety; and (c) Municipal Treasurer pay suppliers in check following the
general principles of the imprest system in handling cash.
The Management assured strict adherence to R.A No. 9184 and its Revised IRR
for all procurement activity in the ensuing year. They also promised to strictly follow
COA Circular No. 2012-001 and COA Circular No. 97-002.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, the Solid Waste Management
Board, the Municipal Planning and Development Officer and all the department
heads involved in the implementation of Solid Waste Management Plan adopt
measures to promote and ensure the effective implementation of the Looc
Ecological Solid Waste Management Plan such as (a) close coordination with
Barangay Officials relative to the segregation at source policy; (b) establishment of
Material Recovery Facility; (c) information dissemination of the program; and (d)
educating waste generators on the necessity and the importance of waste
management.

The Municipal Mayor commented during the exit conference  that  the
   Inventory  Committee  is  still  in  the   process   of   preparing   the
   corresponding inventory reports.

        We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor:

        a. require the Inventory Committee to conduct the complete physical
           count of all Inventories and PPE accounts of the Municipality so
           that the corresponding reports can be  submitted  to  the  Audit
           Team;

        b. thereafter, instruct the Municipal Accountant to  reconcile  the
           resulting balance of the  physical  count  with  the  accounting
           records. In  the  event  that  deficiencies/discrepancies  exist
           between  the  Inventory  Report  and  accounting  records,   the
           necessary adjustments/corrections should be made; and
        c.  require  the  Municipal  Treasurer  to   make   a   report   of
           unserviceable/non-functional  PPEs   in   the   Inspection   and
           Inventory Report of Unserviceable Properties (IIRUP) which could
           be undertaken  simultaneous  with  the  physical  inventory  for
           disposal and eventual proper relief of accountabilities.

Based  on   the   foregoing   observations,   we   reiterated   our
   recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal  Accountant
   to:

        a. exert extra effort to account the unreconciled items of two  (2)
           PNB accounts appearing in the Bank Reconciliation Statements  of
           LBP accounts to faithfully present the correct balances  of  the
           accounts;


        b. prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the erroneous
           entries made in the Cash in Bank account;


        c. revert to proper Cash in Bank and  payable  accounts  all  Stale
           Checks; and


        d. prepare and submit the BRS together with the bank statements and
           paid checks for all the Municipality’s bank accounts within  the
           reglementary period.

The Municipal Mayor  commented  that  portion  of  the  outstanding
   receivables was already collected from the creditors. For those who  have
   not yet settled, the Municipality had already initiated the  issuance  of
   demand letters. They also recalled that they have already  requested  the
   Sangguniang Bayan (SB) to pass a resolution requesting authority from COA
   to write – off the dormant accounts but they eventually lost track of the
   said request. They promised to submit another request to the SB.

        We recommended that the  Municipal  Mayor  instruct  the  Municipal
   Accountant to render report on the status of the demand letters sent  and
   furnish the Audit Team with copies of the demand letters duly received by
   the concerned accountable officers pursuant to COA Memorandum  No.  2017-
   010 dated May 15, 2017.

        We also recommended that local officials expedite the submission of
   the request for  authority  to  write-off  dormant  receivable  accounts,
   unliquidated cash advances, and fund  transfers  of  National  Government
   Agencies (NGAs), Local Government Units (LGUs) and  Government-Owned  and
   Controlled Corporations (GOCCs) to the Commission on Audit in  line  with
   COA Circular No. 2016-005, stating the reasons for write-off  and  extent
   of follow-up made.  The management should see to it that the request  for
   write – off is duly supported with the documents required in Item 8.3  of
   the mentioned circular.

In view of the foregoing audit observations,  we  recommended  that
   the Municipal Mayor direct the:


        a. LDRRMO to prepare and submit the LDRRMFIP to the Audit  Team  in
           accordance with Item 5.1.2 of COA Circular  No.  2012-002  dated
           September 12, 2012;


        b. Submit documents relative to the transfer of the CY 2016 unspent
           LDRRMF for verification of the Audit Team;

        c. Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere with the guidelines  set
           forth by COA Circular No. 2012-002 for proper accounting of the
           utilization and  transfer  of  LDRRM  Fund  as  well  as  proper
           recording of various transactions in the Special Trust Fund  and
           Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular  No.  2012-001  for
           proper documentation of transactions; and

        d. Municipal Accountant to prepare an adjusting  entry  to  correct
           the error noted in the setting-up of inter-fund  receivable  and
           payable.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

       a. instruct the BAC to a) furnish the Audit Team with a copy  of  the
          Contract of Agreement/Purchase Orders and its  attachments  within
          five (5) working days  from  the  execution  of  the  contract  as
          required under Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular  No.  2009-001  dated
          February 23, 2009; b) observe strictly the provisions of the  2016
          Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 to ensure adherence to the principles
          of  transparency,  competitiveness  and  public   monitoring;   c)
          strengthen internal control over the evaluation of  the  bids  and
          post-qualification of bidders by requiring the BAC and the TWG  to
          ensure timeliness, completeness and veracity of the post-qualified

        We have also audited the payments made to contractors  in  CY  2017
   for Project Nos. 1 to 5 and 7 to 9 and one (1) project implemented in  CY
   2017 which is the Rehabilitation of Public Market-Provision of Additional

   1. There were deficiencies  in  the  document  attached  to  the  advance
      payment, progress billings and release  of  retention  money  for  the
      three (3) Infrastructure Projects of the municipality in CY 2017.


           Section 4 (6) of Presidential Decree No. 1445,  otherwise  known
      as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines provides that:


           “Claims  against  government  funds  shall  be  supported   with
             complete documentation.”


           Section 474 b(5) of R.A. No. 7160 provides that it is  the  duty
      of the Municipal Accountant to:


           “(5) Review supporting documents before preparation of  vouchers
             to determine completeness of requirements.”


           Item No. 9.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June  14,  2012
      prescribes the hereunder additional documentary requirements common to
      all infrastructure transactions:


           • Letter from Contractors for advance/progress/final payment  or
             for substitution in case of release of retention money
           • Common to progress/final payments
             - Statement of Work Accomplished/Progress Billing
             - Inspection Report by the Agency's Authorized Engineer
             - Results of Test Analysis, if applicable
             - Statement of Time Elapsed
             - Monthly Certificate of Payment
             - Contractor's Affidavit on payment of laborers and materials
             - Pictures, before, during and after construction of items  of
               work especially the embedded items
             - Photocopy of vouchers of all previous payments
             - Certificate of completion

We also recommended for the submission of the lacking documents for proper evaluation and audit.
observations.managementAction: 2016 SALN were submitted and error in recording of the transaction has been corrected.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the officials and employees who are members of Leagues to require or demand the issuance of an official receipt of the Republic of the Philippines by the recipient Leagues in order to support the release of these funds, pursuant to Section 69 of the GAAM, Volume I, and to facilitate the audit of leagues pursuant to COA Resolution No. 2011-014.
observations.managementAction: Management requested for the issuance of official receipt of the Republic of the Philippines for payment of dues and registration fees to various leagues.
observations.reason: Some leagues have no official receipt of the Republic of the Philippines.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the General Services Officer secure insurance coverage with the Property Insurance Fund of the GSIS for all insurable assets of the Municipality to guarantee its indemnification/compensation on in case of losses/damages due to theft, calamities or any unforeseen events.
observations.managementAction: The GSO already sent a request for inspection of all insurable properties which is to be conducted by GSIS or its authorized representative for assessment and validation.
observations.reason: Inspection of all insurable properties which is to be conducted by GSIS or its authorized representative has not yet been done.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the officers concerned to implement the projects promptly in accordance with the specific purpose of the trust fund in order that the constituents could enjoy the benefits of the government's programs and projects in due time.
observations.managementAction: Started the implementation of the following projects: Concreting of Road Leading to Municipal Port – Agkawayan Potable Water Supply Construction of Evacuation Centers (Bulacan, Balikyas, Talaotao and Ambil)
observations.reason: Project implementation was delayed due to lack of interested Suppliers and Contractors.

observations.managementAction: The Municipality implemented developmental projects and increased its project implementation and registered a 3.43% utilization rate compared with last year’s 0.37%.
observations.reason: Some projects need significant amount, thus they are compounding and accumulating their funds.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the optimal utilization of the LDRRMF to strengthen the resilience of the constituents to calamities and climate change impacts. We also recommended that the unexpended LDRRM Fund be transferred to Special Trust Fund to be utilized solely for supporting disaster risk reduction and management activities pursuant to RA No. 10121 and its IRR.
observations.managementAction: The unexpended balance of LDRRMF was already transferred to the Trust Fund books.
observations.reason: Management intends to utilize the unutilized fund in the ensuing year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Mayor require the GAD Focal Person to synchronize the preparation of GAD Plan and Budget with the planning and budgeting calendar to enable its prompt submission to the PPDO and then to the DILG Regional Office for review and approval before its incorporation in the annual budget to be enacted by the local Sanggunian. We also recommended for strict compliance with the other provisions of PCW/DILG/DBM/NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2017, the GAD Plan and Budget was already reviewed and approved by the DILG.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management exhaust all the available remedies provided by the rules and regulations in enforcing settlement of suspensions, disallowances, and charges in accordance with COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2017, settlements were made for suspensions, disallowances and charges amounting to P323,203.39, P58,800.00, and P8,180.00, respectively.
observations.reason: No reason was submitted for its failure to settle the audit suspensions.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

require the Inventory Committee to conduct the complete physical count of all Inventories and PPE accounts of the Municipality so that the corresponding reports can be submitted to the Audit Team; 

thereafter, instruct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the resulting balance of the physical count with the accounting records. In the event that deficiencies/discrepancies exist between the Inventory Report and accounting records, the necessary adjustments/corrections should be made; and

require the Municipal Treasurer to make a report of unserviceable/non-functional PPEs in the Inspection and Inventory Report of Unserviceable Properties (IIRUP) which could be undertaken simultaneous with the physical inventory for disposal and eventual proper relief of accountabilities.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor has already assigned one LGU personnel to focus on the preparation of the inventory reports.
observations.reason: The RPCI and RPCPPE for CY 2016 and 2017 were not yet submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.managementAction: P0.50 error was corrected on January 3, 2017 under JEV No. 2017-01-000036.
observations.reason: For CY 2017, there are still reconciling items that need to be adjusted in the books. “Float items” continue to exist in the BRS and same observations were noted for stale checks and delayed submission of BRS.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

instruct the BAC to a) furnish the Audit Team with a copy of the Contract of Agreement and its attachments within five (5) working days from the execution of the contract as required under Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 23, 2009; b) observe strictly the provisions of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 and the new provisions of 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 to ensure adherence to the principles of transparency, competitiveness and public monitoring; c) strengthen internal control over the evaluation of the bids and post-qualification of bidders by requiring the BAC and the TWG to ensure timeliness, completeness and veracity of the post-qualified documents as reported in the Bid and Post- Qualification Evaluation Reports; and d) submit the lacking documents cited above to the Audit Team to comply with the requirements of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 and address the deficiencies enumerated; and

instruct the Municipal Engineer to observe DPWH D.O. 22 dated February 18, 2015 which provides the guidelines in the preparation of the Approved Budget for the Contract.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, as the internal auditor, see to it that supporting documents attached to the vouchers are accurate and complete before processing the claim. 

We also recommended that management be guided with the provisions of Section 6.2 of Annex E of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 on the release of Retention Money.
observations.managementAction: BAC Resolution Recommending Award of Contract was already prepared. Conduct of bid evaluation and post-qualification were already within time frame.
observations.reason: For the CY 2017 contracts reviewed by the Audit Team, there were same lapses/ deficiencies that were noted.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the foregoing audit observations, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

Local Disaster Risk and Reduction Council (LDRRMC) to submit the LDRRMF Investment Plan for CY 2016 and henceforth submit the required reports such as LDRRMF Investment Plan and Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF regularly to the Audit Team; and

Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere with guidelines set forth by COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 for proper accounting on the utilization and transfer of LDRRM Fund and COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015 for proper recording of various transactions.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRMFIP for CY 2017 was submitted.
observations.reason: There was no actual cash fund transfer made for the unexpended balance of LDRRM Fund of CY 2016. Lapses in the documentations and recording were still noted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management revisit the SWM Plan and revise it by incorporating all the requirements prescribed in Section 17 of R.A. No. 9003 and also consider the other requirements of the NSWMC, and submit the same to the NSWMC for final approval.



We recommended that the Management fast track the processing of various requirements for the operation of a sanitary landfill in accordance with Sections 40 and 41 of R.A. No. 9003 the soonest possible time to avert adverse environmental consequences.
observations.managementAction: The municipality uses the alternative method of waste management such as the use of MRF which is being regularly checked/ inspected by the Environmental Management Bureau.
observations.reason: No revision or update on the Municipal’s ten-year Solid Waste Management (SWM) Plan was made for CY 2017 and there was still no sanitary landfill established.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to render report on the status of the demand letters sent to enforce collection thereof to protect the interest of the government. 

We further reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Accountant expedite the re-submission of the request for authority to write-off dormant accounts to the Commission on Audit in line with COA Resolution No. 2003-002, dated January 30, 2003, stating the reasons for write-off and extent of follow-up made.  They should also see to it that the request for write – off is duly supported with the following documents:

List of available records and information as to the number of years these accounts have been non-moving; 

Copies of Demand Letters issued; and

Certification as to the extent of validation made by the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant on the accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor commented that there were already payments made by the creditors. They have also issued demand letters to the accountable officers.
observations.reason: The long outstanding receivables totaling P809,200.00 continued to exist in the books as of December 31, 2017.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the additional documents enumerated in the letter of the Audit Team dated December 23, 2015 be submitted.  Also, we stressed out that each accountable officer must submit request for relief from accountability in compliance with Section 73 of PD No. 1445 to facilitate the Audit Team’s evaluation and recommendation.  Otherwise, these accountable officers shall not be relieved from accountability or be allowed credit for the loss during the settlement of his/her account.
observations.managementAction: Additional documents for the request for relief from accountability of Dr. Mary Ann B. Gado was submitted and the request was eventually granted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant, as the internal auditor of the agency, to see to it that disbursement vouchers are completely supported with valid documents before processing the claims.  Likewise, instruct also the BAC to observe strictly the provisions of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 regarding the use of alternative modes of procurement.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2017, most of the required documents for purchases of goods and services were already attached to disbursement vouchers.
observations.reason: There were still lapses in the preparation of purchase orders such as the dates of supplier’s confirmation were not indicated in the documents. Non – compliance with the conditions/ requirements in the use of the alternative modes of procurement was still noted.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the BAC Secretariat, or other officer assigned as the case may be, ensure the timely submission of copies of government contracts and each of all the documents forming part thereof within five (5) working days from execution and purchase orders within five (5) working days from issuance, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009, to ensure timely review and evaluation of same, so that the management could be immediately notified of any defects/deficiencies noted, if any.
observations.managementAction: Submission of contracts and purchase orders to the Audit Team were already implemented.
observations.reason: Most of the contracts and purchase orders were submitted beyond the five (5) working days requirement.

observations.managementAction: Cash accountability of Ms. Nida Garcia which was accounted in the Cash Collecting Officer account was deposited on June 19, 2017. Issuance of official receipts for bank to bank payment of Looc National High School were already stopped. Discrepancies in the calculation of collections were minimized.
observations.reason: Undeposited collection as of as of December 31, 2017 amounted to P150,592.32.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and Municipal Accountant adhere strictly to the provisions of COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015 on the allocation and utilization of Confidential/Intelligence Fund. Submit the required lacking documents to prove the validity, propriety and regularity of the cash advances and submit the liquidation report completely supported by the required documents to the ICFAU to avoid the unnecessary action the Commission might execute relative to the above deficiencies.

We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant be guided by COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015 for proper recording of various transactions.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2017, the municipality refrained from incurring additional cash advances for Confidential and Intelligence Fund which could not meet the requirements set forth by the existing guidelines in the utilization of the fund.
observations.reason: Required documents to liquidate the previous cash advances were not yet submitted to COA-ICFAU.

observations.recommendation:
Based on the foregoing observations, we recommended the following:

The Municipal Mayor along with all the concerned officials and employees strictly follow the provisions of DILG-DBM in planning the programs/projects/activities funded by 20% Development Fund and their implementation. See to it that those development projects identified in the AIP were fully implemented for the benefit of the constituents.

The Municipal Accountant properly classify each transaction to the appropriate accounts as provided by COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015.  Explain also the discrepancy between the List of Vouchers Charged Against 20% Development Fund and Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts. Henceforth, proper coordination be made with concerned offices in order to generate accurate and reliable reports.
observations.managementAction: According to the Municipal Budget Officer, in the preparation of the CY 2018 20% Development Fund, the municipality ensured that all the projects to be charged against the fund are capital in nature except for the operation and maintenance of Materials Recovery Facility. But they promised that this will be eventually eliminated.
observations.reason: Several 20% developmental projects were still unimplemented as of December 31, 2017. Several projects which do not partake the nature of capital investments were still charged to the fund during the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management enforce the preparation of PPMPs by end-users for consolidation in the APP. See to it that the APP is consistent with the Annual Budget, reflects the entire procurement activity of the agency for calendar year and have all necessary information required under GPPB Circular 1-2009 dated 20 January 2009.
observations.reason: Preparation of PPMP by end-user units has not yet been implemented during the year. Incomplete APP was submitted by the Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management strictly adhere with the Supreme Court’s decision and remit any unutilized PDAF to the unappropriated surplus of the general fund. (i.e. National Treasury).
observations.reason:
The remaining PDAF balance was not remitted to the Bureau of Treasury.
Management intends to utilize the remaining fund in the ensuing year.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant coordinate with Mr. Eufemio Rufon and follow up the status of collections and report/s made by the latter.  We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to send demand letters to enforce collection thereof to protect the interest of the government. 

We further recommended that local officials expedite the re-submission of the request for authority to write-off dormant accounts to the Commission on Audit in line with COA Resolution No. 2003-002, dated January 30, 2003, stating the reasons for write-off and extent of follow-up made.  The management should see to it that the request for write – off is duly supported with the following documents:

List of available records and information as to the number of years these accounts have been non-moving; 

Copies of Demand Letters issued; and

Certification as to the extent of validation made by the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant on the accounts.
observations.reason: The long outstanding receivables totaling P809,200.00 continued to exist in the books as of December 31, 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the BAC to adhere strictly to the provisions of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 in all stages of the procurement process.  Henceforth, ensure that all necessary documents relative to the procurement are properly and completely reviewed by the BAC for submission to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: For the CY 2016 contracts reviewed by the Audit Team, same observations were noted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the BAC to adhere strictly to the provisions of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 in all stages of the procurement process.  Henceforth, ensure that all necessary documents relative to the procurement are properly and completely reviewed by the BAC for submission to the Audit Team.

Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the documentation on the distribution of the assistive devices to PWDs to support the amount debited to Donations (Account 878). observations.reason:
For the CY 2016 contracts reviewed by the Audit Team, same observations were noted.

Documents relevant to the distribution of the assistive devices to PWDs as recommended in prior year’s audit were not submitted to the Audit Team.

 Volume I.

 cash advances totaling P1,784,091.59 pursuant  to  the  provisions  of  COA
 Circular No. 97-002. We also recommended that the Management facilitate the
 completion of documents for the write off of unliquidated cash  advance  of
 the deceased Rural Health Physician.

 MSWDO and the MAO to exert their best effort  to  collect  long-outstanding
 receivables. We also recommended that the Municipal  Accountant update their

 ledger balance of Due from NGOs/POs account to ensure correctness of the

        a. require the Inventory Committee to conduct the complete physical

   adhere strictly to the provisions of COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND Joint  Circular

   prove the validity, propriety and regularity of  the  cash  advances  and
   submit the  liquidation  report  completely  supported  by  the  required

We recommended that Management, with the initiative of Municipal Mayor, give their best effort to collect long-outstanding receivables.

observations.managementAction:
Collections are done thru the Barangay Treasurer to ensure that outstanding receivables are collected from their respective Barangays.
Collected total amount of P199,114.00 during the year.
observations.reason: Borrowers cannot pay their full obligations due to other priorities of the family; crops were also damaged by typhoon.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:
• the Municipal Accountant ensure that claims against government funds are supported with complete and proper documentations to establish the validity, propriety and regularity of transactions.
• fund transfers be recorded in the books of accounts as receivable requiring liquidation reports from the concerned barangays for the fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements; and
• proper evaluation and monitoring be conducted regarding the Barangay’s implementation and completion of the project/s for which the assistance has been given, as well as the prompt liquidation of financial assistance.
observations.managementAction:
As early as the first quarter of 2016, fund transfer/financial assistance to barangays were already recorded as receivables in the books of accounts.
Liquidations are strictly monitored among Barangay Treasurers.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:
• The Municipal Accountant ensure that payroll/claims are supported with complete and sufficient documents to establish the validity and regularity of the payment for salaries of JO/contractual/consultants;
• The Human Resource Management Officer review the existing personnel complement to establish the necessity of recruitment /hiring/assignment of job order/contractual and consultants to eliminate duplication of functions and maximize the utilization of manpower services; and
• ensure the prompt submission of job order contracts and contract of services together with their resume for evaluation and audit purposes.
observations.managementAction:
A” NO DTR/NO/ACCOMPLISHMENT REPORT/NO PAYROLL” Policy was implemented by the Management.
Hiring was done only when a requisition is received by the HRMO from different heads of offices who are in need of Job Orders personnel.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor install internal controls on the use of government motor vehicles by requiring the following reports from concerned officials and employees to minimize wasteful, excessive and unnecessary expenditures for fuel consumption in accordance with COA Circular No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977:


Driver’s Trip Ticket
Requisition Issue Voucher or its Equivalent
Daily Gas Issue Record
Summary of Requisition and Issue Vouchers or its Equivalent
Gasoline Control Card
Monthly Report of Official Travels
Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption.
observations.managementAction: Necessary reports in accordance with COA Circular No.77-61 dated Sept. 26, 1977 are now being accomplished starting January of 2016.

observations.recommendation: We therefore recommended that the Municipal Accountant compute correctly the RATA to be granted to members of Sanggunian based on their actual attendance to regular sessions in accordance with Section 7.5 of DBM Local Budget Circular No. 103.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2016, the Management granted RATA of SB members based on their actual attendance to regular sessions.

observations.recommendation:

Local Disaster Risk and Reduction Council (LDRRMC) to submit LDDRM Investment Plan and Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF; and

Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere with guidelines set forth by COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 for proper accounting on the utilization and transfer of LDRRM Fund.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

The Municipal Treasurer strictly observe the existing rules and regulations governing collections and deposits of government funds to avoid risk of improper use or possible loss thereof; and

The Municipal Accountant ensure the accuracy of the amounts of the recorded transactions and adjust the balance of the accounts affected by the errors in computations, as appropriate.
observations.managementAction: The delay incurred for CY 2016 remittances was significantly reduced by 284 days.
observations.reason: Delay in remittances as long as 83 days was still noted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant, as the internal auditor of the agency, to see to it that disbursement vouchers are completely supported with valid documents before processing the claims.  Likewise, instruct also the BAC to comply strictly with the provisions of Section 52.1 of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2016, some of the required documents were attached to the disbursement vouchers.
observations.reason: There are still disbursement vouchers which were not completely supported with the required documents as observed in prior year.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant:

give priority to the timely submission of the accounts within the first ten (10) days of the ensuing month as required under COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 and COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995 to enable the Audit Team to conduct timely audit thereof and promptly inform the management of the deficiencies noted, if any; and

submit the unsubmitted disbursement vouchers. Likewise, ensure the completeness of the DVs including their supporting documents before submitting same to the Office of the Auditor in order to facilitate the audit of the transactions and enable us to ascertain the legality, validity and propriety of the vouchers, otherwise said transactions will be suspended or disallowed in audit, as the case maybe.
observations.managementAction: The municipality hired two (2) additional accounting staff during the year
observations.reason: Significant delay was still incurred by the Accounting Office in the submission of CY 2016 accounts and reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC Secretariat, or other officer assigned as the case may be, ensure the timely submission of copies of government contracts and each of all the documents forming part thereof within five (5) working days from execution and purchase orders within five (5) working days from issuance, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009, to ensure timely review and evaluation of same, so that the management could be immediately notified of any defects/deficiencies noted, if any.

observations.reason: Majority of the Purchase Orders/Contracts received by the Audit Team were not submitted within the period prescribed by COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Planning and Development Officer furnish the Audit Team with a copy of the Annual Investment Plan for CY 2015.  Moreover, concerned officials ensure that all projects chargeable to 20% Development Fund be in accordance with DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.
observations.managementAction: The Annual Investment Plan for CY 2016 was furnished to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the agency allocate no less than five percent (5%) of the total budgets for GAD.  The agency should develop its own implementing guidelines for the AICS program to ensure that financial assistance shall be given to bonafide beneficiaries.  Strict compliance to Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA must always be observed.
observations.managementAction: The CY 2016 reviewed GAD Plan and Budget together with the recommendations of DILG Region IV-B was submitted to the Audit Team. The municipality has also properly appropriated the budgetary requirement for GAD in CY 2016.
observations.reason: The municipality still failed to develop its own implementing guidelines in providing cash assistance or AICS. Discrepancies in GAD related reports were still noted.

observations.recommendation: We therefore reiterated our previous years’ recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to remit the excess and unutilized PDAF funds to the Bureau of Treasury and ask Official Receipt for the same pursuant to Supreme Court decisions declaring PDAF acts and practices unconstitutional and in compliance to Section 6.7 of COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994.
observations.managementAction: Management has remitted the remaining DAP on April 21, 2016. While the remaining PDAF was disbursed in July 2016.
